insight: tfc_lane returns effectiveLane=blocked/reachable=false until tfc_install creates the managed CLAUDE_SKILLS symlink, even when the eval passes 1.0. Proven and reachable are two separate gates; do not report a freshly-evaled skill as live before install.

The voice-em-dash and no-absolute-paths validators fire on the managed TFC:PREAMBLE-HOOK block and the ${TFC_HOME:-$HOME/.future-code} portable fallback in bash blocks. A skill author cannot fix these (the hook is regenerated by tfc_install) and should not (removing $HOME breaks portability). They are gate false-positives on managed machinery, not authored-voice defects; ultraflow stays 96/100 honestly.
forge route to eval_proven in one pass; new category ai-image; voice gate docks points for the banned-words list itself and em-dash phase headers, fix authored prose to reach 100.

When forging an existing skill (v4→v8), reading the current file (v7.0) first is essential — it was already gap-first; the real improvement was making it self-contained with embedded SM-2 and inline-first output contract

enhance-a-proven-skill routes to BUILD/enhance-in-place not FORGE; a multi-axis make-it-better ask (better+optimized+capable) often collapses to ONE mechanism (parallel diverse-lens tournament). Dogfooding the new gate mode caught a real delta-extraction bug in the author draft (read median not delta) — gate-as-architecture verify is non-optional.

CCE v2 added 4 new context tools not yet in ultraflow TFC Tool Map: tfc_context_get (ranked retrieval for stage-4 seeding), tfc_context_fill (offline grounded fill prompt), tfc_context_discover (find domains from disk), tfc_context_coverage (angle-completeness audit). tfc_context_get is stage-4 material, not just govern.
